messages.content: Can you modify the previously defined Material:Cleanup method to make correct use of this new Shader::Cleanup method, or provide new code that properly links the two?
It seems I am now getting errors in the Material::LoadShaders method. I believe the call to the Cleanup method needs to be adjusted when creating the shaders. Can you modify the code accordingly?
It seems this hasn't resolved the error. It is still throwing an error saying there is no instance of constructor. Could there be something else that's wrong?
It seems it's still throwing an error. This time it seems to be related to the Shader::Cleanup part of the shader creation. It seemd to suggest it is expecting a pointer instead of a sharded pointer.
It seems this has just shifted the error from the LoadShaders method to the ShaderDeleter struct. The error occurs at this line:  Shader::Cleanup(std::shared_ptr<Shader>(shaderPtr, {}));
The new ShaderDeleter struct is exactly the same as the previous version and, as such, does not resolve the error.

It still seems to be having the issue. I belive the shaderTemp is expecting shaderPtr to be a shared_ptr instead of a pointer. It either needs to convert from the pointer input into a shared_ptr or the other sections of the code need to be modified to accept a shared_ptr type.
